title: Published teaching collection / course pack + unifying guide
context: By M3 the project will have shipped >= 6 datasets across >= 3 skill areas. Without a unifying collection guide, educators must discover and assemble these individually. A published teaching collection / course pack bundles the datasets with a unifying guide: a curriculum map (which dataset teaches which skill, at what difficulty), suggested learning progressions (e.g. beginner → intermediate), instructor notes for multi-dataset courses, and a single landing document that an educator can cite and adopt as a course pack. Depends on ds-expr-013, ds-clean-015, and ds-incidence-017 being shipped.
objective: Write and publish a unifying teaching collection guide that presents the shipped datasets as a coherent course pack with a curriculum map, learning progressions, and instructor notes for adopting multiple datasets.
acceptanceCriteria: Curriculum map covers all shipped datasets: dataset name, teaching skill, difficulty level, prerequisites, and suggested use (standalone lesson or progression).
At least 2 suggested learning progressions (e.g. 'beginner cancer data science track', 'bioinformatics methods track') that sequence datasets across skill areas.
Instructor notes section: how to integrate multiple datasets into a course, timing estimates, platform requirements, and common student misconceptions.
A single citable landing document (Zenodo DOI or equivalent) that an educator can reference as 'the open-teaching-datasets course pack'.
Safety notice present; all dataset attributions and licenses recorded; collection guide license CC-BY-4.0.
Educator reviewer confirms the collection guide is pedagogically coherent and adoption-ready; document committed; commit DCO signed-off.
resources: PLAN.md (§Roadmap M3 — teaching collection / course pack)
output: A published teaching collection guide / course pack document (Markdown + Zenodo DOI) presenting all shipped datasets as a coherent, educator-adoptable curriculum.
